settings.title: Our Sourdough Is Now Baked Every Day
settings.excerpt: After two years of weekends only, the long-ferment loaf has earned a permanent spot in the oven.
settings.body:
We started baking sourdough at Crumbly on Saturdays as a small experiment. It sold out so fast that we have decided to make it a daily loaf.

The dough ferments for around twenty-four hours, which is why the crust shatters and the crumb stays open and chewy. Nothing goes in but flour, water, salt, and time.

Loaves come out of the oven around nine each morning. Once the day's batch is gone, it is gone, so the early risers tend to win.
